ocr.text: The analysis of Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) and wind farm capacity factors within the Asia-Pacific region highlights
the evolving dynamics of renewable energy pricing and operational efficiency.
Category  PricingSummary  TotalRevenue  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  AveragePrice(KRW)
Type  Q12024  Q12023  Change(%)  ForecastPrice  ActualPrice  ContractVolume(MWh)
Solar  50,000  [HIDDEN]  11.1  20.5  [HIDDEN]  200,000
Wind  30,000  [HIDDEN]  7.1  [HIDDEN]  16.0  120,000
As evidenced in the data presented, the wind sector reported a significant average capacity factor (AverageCF) of 200% for
WindFarmB, indicating robust performance relative to its design expectations. In contrast, the solar sector, which had an
ActualPrice of 22.0 KRW per MWh, demonstrates a competitive landscape, as seen in its previous quarter where it achieved a
total production volume of 45,000 MWh. The quarterly comparison reveals that the wind projects experienced a measured
change in capacity factors, with Wind showing an operational capacity of 28,000 MWh during Q12023. Furthermore, the
forecasted price for future wind energy contracts stands at 15.2 KRW, suggesting an anticipated upward trend in the market.
WindFarmOverview  OperationalMetrics
ProjectName  CapacityMetrics  Region
TotalCapacity(MW)  AverageCF(%)  UnitsProduced(MWh)  OperationalDays  MaintenanceHours  Revenue(KRW)  CO2Savings(tons)
WindFarmB  [HIDDEN]  42.0  [HIDDEN]  85  40  35,000,000
Operational metrics such as maintenance hours, exemplified by WindFarmD's 88 hours, and the extensive operational days
recorded by WindFarmB at 170,000 days, further illustrate the commitment to maximizing efficiency in energy production.
Collectively, these metrics underscore the importance of strategic planning and investment in the renewable energy sector to
enhance sustainability and economic viability across the region.
